methods.description: This method describes the process for extracting total protein from plant ovary samples, separating proteins using 2D-PAGE (SDS-PAGE followed by isoelectric focusing and a second dimension separation), detecting and quantifying protein spots, and identifying differential proteins using MALDI-TOF/TOF mass spectrometry and database searching.

steps.description: The SP and OP ovaries at 48 h were frozen and ground to power.
steps.searchable_summary: freeze and grind plant tissue samples

steps.description: 1 g of the sample was used for protein extraction.
steps.searchable_summary: extract protein from ground plant tissue

steps.description: Then, 10 μg samples were run on 12% SDS-PAGE gel.
steps.searchable_summary: run protein samples on SDS-PAGE gel

steps.description: visualized by CBB stain.
steps.searchable_summary: stain SDS-PAGE gel with Coomassie Brilliant Blue
outputs: Stained SDS-PAGE gel with visible protein bands

steps.description: Gel images with 300 dots per inch were scanned using an image scanner (GE Healthcare, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: scan stained gel images

steps.description: A total of 450 μl solution with 1500 μg protein sample was used to finish isoelectric focusing with the following parameters: 50uA per strip, rehydration at 50 V for 8 h, 100 V for 1 h, 200 V for 1 h, 500 V for 1 h, 1000 V for 1 h, 1000-10,000 V (gradient) for 1 h, 10,000 V for 13 h, 500 V for 12 h, temperature, 20 °C.
steps.searchable_summary: perform isoelectric focusing (IEF) on protein sample
inputs: Protein sample (1500 μg in 450 μl solution)
outputs: Proteins separated by isoelectric point on IEF strip

steps.description: The Ettan-DALT-Six system was run for 45 min at 100 V and then at 300 V for 6-8 h.
steps.searchable_summary: perform second dimension separation using Ettan-DALT-Six system
outputs: Proteins separated by molecular weight on 2D gel

steps.description: Using PDquest 8.0 software, all gel images were processed in three steps: spot detection, volumetric quantification, and matching.
steps.searchable_summary: process 2D gel images for spot detection, quantification, and matching using PDquest software

steps.description: The differential protein spots were selected using two thresholds (p ≤ 0.05, fold change ≥ 2 or ≤ 0.5).
steps.searchable_summary: select differential protein spots based on statistical significance and fold change thresholds

steps.description: An ABI 5800 MALDI-TOF/TOF Plus mass spectrometer (Applied Biosystems, Foster City, USA) was used for peptide MS and MS/MS detection.
steps.searchable_summary: perform peptide mass spectrometry (MS) and tandem mass spectrometry (MS/MS) using MALDI-TOF/TOF

steps.description: Cal-Mix5 was used to calibrate the instrument (ABI5800 Calibration Mixture).
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate mass spectrometer using standard mixture

steps.description: GPS Explorer V3.6 software (Applied Biosystems, USA) with default parameters was used for data integration.
steps.searchable_summary: integrate mass spectrometry data using GPS Explorer software
outputs: Integrated MS/MS data for protein identification

steps.description: the proteins were identified using the MASCOT V2.3 search engine (Matrix Science Ltd., London, U.K.).
steps.searchable_summary: identify proteins by searching mass spectrometry data against a database using MASCOT search engine

methods.description: This method describes the process of integrating and comparing transcriptome and proteome data, including identifying corresponding genes/proteins, performing correlation analysis, GO enrichment analysis, and visualizing results using heatmaps.

steps.description: The corresponding transcripts were identified using the gene ID of the proteome, and the corresponding relationship between the protein and the transcript was determined.
steps.searchable_summary: map protein identifiers to corresponding transcript identifiers
inputs: List of identified proteins (with gene IDs)

steps.description: Correlation analysis was conducted on the fold change (taken as log2) of genes (proteins) identified by transcriptome and proteome analyses in the two omics stydies.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate correlation between log2 fold changes of corresponding genes and proteins
inputs: Log2 fold change values for genes (transcriptome)
Log2 fold change values for corresponding proteins (proteome)

steps.description: The drawing process was implemented using Python.
steps.searchable_summary: generate plots for correlation analysis results using Python

steps.description: The different multiples of the corresponding transcriptome genes were determined in the two omics studies. The difference multiples were taken as log2.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate log2 fold change for corresponding transcriptome genes
inputs: Transcriptome expression data for corresponding genes
outputs: Log2 fold change values for corresponding transcriptome genes

steps.description: then plotted using the ComplexHeatmap of the R package.
steps.searchable_summary: generate heatmap of log2 fold changes for corresponding transcriptome genes using ComplexHeatmap R package
inputs: Log2 fold change values for corresponding transcriptome genes

steps.description: GO annotation of the transcriptome and proteome was used for GO function enrichment using the tool wego (http:// wego. genom ics. org. cn/).
steps.searchable_summary: perform GO function enrichment analysis using wego tool with transcriptome and proteome GO annotations

steps.description: Clustering heat map analysis of GO/KEGG function enrichment was conducted using the fold change (taken as log2) of DEPs identified using the proteome and corresponding transcriptome genes.
steps.searchable_summary: perform clustering heatmap analysis of GO/KEGG enrichment using log2 fold changes of DEPs and corresponding DEGs
inputs: Log2 fold change of corresponding DEGs (transcriptome)

steps.description: The drawing process was implemented using ComplexHeatmap.
steps.searchable_summary: generate clustering heatmap of GO/KEGG enrichment using ComplexHeatmap

methods.description: This method describes the source, handling, and preparation of plant materials (cultivars 'JO59' and 'YX1'), the procedure for performing self-pollination (SP) and outcross-pollination (OP), and the collection and storage of ovary and style samples at various time points after pollination.

steps.description: Two cultivars, 'JO59' and 'YX1' , grown in Lanxi nursery, Zhejiang province, China, were used in this study.
steps.searchable_summary: select and obtain specific plant cultivars

steps.description: The scions were collected from selected trees of the natural forest of S. superba from Jianou and Youxi in Fujian; they were then grafted on local stock and maintained in Lanxi until 2013.
steps.searchable_summary: collect scions, graft onto stock, and maintain plants

steps.description: In May 2019, at 9:00-11:00 am on sunny days, we performed pollination [self-pollination (SP) was 'JO59' × 'JO59' , outcross-pollination (OP) was 'JO59' × 'YX1'].
steps.searchable_summary: perform controlled pollination (self and outcross) on plants

steps.description: The styles and ovaries were collected at different intervals (2,4,8,12,16,24,36,48,60,72,84,96, and 120 h after pollination).
steps.searchable_summary: collect plant tissues (styles and ovaries) at multiple time points after pollination

steps.description: Then, the styles and ovaries of each sample with three replications were frozen in liquid nitrogen.
steps.searchable_summary: flash freeze collected plant samples in liquid nitrogen

steps.description: and stored at − 80 °C for enzyme activity, hormone, amino acid, protein, and RNA-seq analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: store frozen plant samples at -80°C

methods.description: This method describes the procedures for measuring the enzymatic activity of three defense-related enzymes: peroxidase (POD), catalase (CAT), and superoxide dismutase (SOD) in plant ovary samples.

steps.description: Three defense-related enzymes, peroxidase (POD), catalase (CAT), and superoxide dismutase (SOD), were analyzed using SP and OP ovaries from 24 to 120 h.
steps.searchable_summary: select plant ovary samples for enzyme activity analysis

steps.description: POD activity was determined using the guaiacol-catalase activity method 55 .
steps.searchable_summary: measure peroxidase (POD) enzyme activity using guaiacol method

steps.description: SOD activity was measured using inhibition in the photoreduction of www.nature.com/scientificreports/ nitro blue tetrazolium (NBT) 56 .
steps.searchable_summary: measure superoxide dismutase (SOD) enzyme activity using NBT photoreduction inhibition method

steps.description: CAT activity was measured spectrophotometrically by monitoring the decrease in absorbance at 240 nm 57 .
steps.searchable_summary: measure catalase (CAT) enzyme activity spectrophotometrically by monitoring absorbance decrease at 240 nm

methods.method_name: Hormone Extraction and Determination by HPLC-MS/MS
methods.description: This method describes the process for extracting plant hormones (auxin, abscisic acid, zeatin) from ovary samples and quantifying their levels using High-Performance Liquid Chromatography coupled with tandem Mass Spectrometry (HPLC-MS/MS).

steps.description: The SP and OP ovaries (0.1 g fresh weight), with three biological replicates from 24 to 120 h, were selected to extract the hormone.
steps.searchable_summary: select plant ovary samples for hormone extraction
outputs: Ovary samples (0.1g fresh weight) for hormone analysis

steps.description: and HPLC-MS/MS analysis was performed (Agilent 1290).
steps.searchable_summary: extract hormones from plant ovary samples

steps.description: HPLC analysis was carried out using a poroshell 120 SB-C18 column (2.1 mm × 150 mm; 2.7 μm). The parameters were set as follows: spray voltage, + 4500 V; automizing temperature, 400 °C; the pressure of the air curtain, nebulizer, and aux gas were 15, 65, and 70 psi, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: perform HPLC-MS/MS analysis with specified column and parameters

steps.description: SAS v8.0 (SAS Institute, Cary, NC, USA) was used to analyze the data.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze HPLC-MS/MS data using SAS software

methods.description: This method describes the process for analyzing the transcriptome of plant ovary samples, including RNA extraction, quality control, library preparation, sequencing, raw data processing, transcript assembly, gene annotation, expression estimation, and differential expression analysis.

steps.description: The total RNA from the S48 and O48 ovaries was extracted using an RNAprep pure Plant Kit (Dingguo, Beijing, China).
steps.searchable_summary: extract total RNA from plant ovary samples using a commercial kit

steps.description: RNA quality and quantity were verified using 1% agarose gels and Agilent 2100 (Agilent Technologies, CA, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: verify RNA quality and quantity using agarose gel electrophoresis and bioanalyzer

steps.description: The mRNA was enriched and purified by magnetic beads with oligo T (dT), cleaved and synthesized first-strand cDNA using random hexamers.
steps.searchable_summary: enrich mRNA, fragment RNA, and synthesize first-strand cDNA for sequencing library preparation

steps.description: The double-strand cDNA libraries were then purified using the AMPure XP system (Beckman Coulter, Beverly, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: synthesize double-strand cDNA and purify sequencing libraries

steps.description: Transcriptome sequencing was performed using the Illumina HiSeq™ 2000 sequencing platform in Novogene (Tianjin, China).
steps.searchable_summary: perform transcriptome sequencing using Illumina platform

steps.description: The adaptor and low-quality sequences (Q < 20 or less than 35 bp) were removed in raw reads.
steps.searchable_summary: trim sequencing adaptors and filter low-quality reads

steps.description: Then the clean reads were assembled using Trinity assembler 59 .
steps.searchable_summary: assemble clean sequencing reads into transcripts using Trinity

steps.description: The functions of the unigenes were annotated using Blastx searches with 1e-5 against the protein databases.
steps.searchable_summary: annotate assembled transcripts by searching against protein databases using Blastx

steps.description: Blast2GO software 60 was used for gene Ontology (GO) term analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Gene Ontology (GO) term analysis using Blast2GO

steps.description: The plant Transcription Factor Database (PlnTFDB) (version 3.0) was used to determine the transcription factors (TFs) 61 .
steps.searchable_summary: identify transcription factors (TFs) using Plant Transcription Factor Database (PlnTFDB)

steps.description: FPKM was used to estimate the expression of each gene 62 .
steps.searchable_summary: estimate gene expression levels using FPKM method

steps.description: and the DESeq R package 1.10.1 was used to estimate the differential expression between the two treatments 63 .
steps.searchable_summary: estimate differential gene expression between treatments using DESeq R package
outputs: Differential expression analysis results (fold changes, p-values)

steps.description: DEGs were determined with an adjusted p value < 0.05 determined by DESeq.
steps.searchable_summary: identify differentially expressed genes (DEGs) based on adjusted p-value threshold

steps.description: The R-seq data were uploaded to the sequence read archive (accession no. SUB6596208).
steps.searchable_summary: upload RNA-seq data to public repository (SRA)
